_readme: TEMPLATE FILE — the fire-code standard has not been confirmed for this client's country/region yet (your sample floor plan referenced NFPA-13 for sprinkler pipe sizing, but that may not apply to every client). Do NOT use these values for real recommendations. Once the applicable standard is known (e.g. NFPA 13/72, NBC 2016 Part 4, a local fire authority code), fill in every 'value' / 'divisor' field below, update 'standard', set 'is_template' to false, and either restart the server or call POST /rules/reload.

rules.notes: Placeholder — set 'value' (minimum room area to trigger this rule) and 'divisor' (sqft of coverage per detector) once the standard is confirmed.
Placeholder — large rooms typically need at least one extinguisher; confirm the area threshold and quantity.
Placeholder — confirm maximum spacing between detectors along a corridor.
Placeholder — every staircase generally needs emergency lighting; confirm count per landing/floor.
Placeholder — exits typically need a visible illuminated sign; usually 1 unless it's a double exit.
Placeholder — building-wide rule, not tied to a single space. Confirm whether tall/large buildings need more than one panel.
Placeholder — your reference floor plan shows fire hose cabinets (FHC) being used; confirm at what building height/area these become mandatory and how many.
